refined_fr.refined_fr: Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional obligations: (1) terrain analysis, (2) obstacle identification, and (3) work-zone representation. Each is a separate concern with different data sources, processing logic, and stakeholder consumption patterns. The refined FR is solution-neutral (avoids 'mapping/modeling' terminology which implies implementation choice) and focuses on the core problem: making spatial features available in a structured, reusable form. Atomicity is achieved by deferring the specific feature types (terrain, obstacles, zones) to sub-FRs.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that a worksite model exists in a defined schema/format, contains spatial coordinates and feature classifications, and is accessible to downstream planning and execution functions per the acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-006.1: Acquire terrain elevation and surface properties across the worksite.
FR-NGHE-006.2: Identify and locate static obstacles (structures, vegetation, utilities) within the worksite boundary.
FR-NGHE-006.3: Define and represent designated work zones (operational areas, restricted zones, safety perimeters) in the worksite model.
FR-NGHE-006.4: Persist and version the worksite model for access by planning and execution functions.

candidate_dps: DP1: Acquire spatial feature data through multi-modal sensing (vision, range, inertial) and fuse into a unified coordinate frame; store as a structured geometric model (point clouds, meshes, or voxel grids) with semantic labels and metadata; expose via query interface for downstream planning/execution consumers.

DP2: Decompose worksite into discrete zones; assign each zone a feature inventory (obstacles, hazards, access points) via manual annotation or semi-automated detection; represent as a graph of zone nodes with edge constraints; persist in a queryable schema.

DP3: Capture worksite features as a rule-based symbolic representation (e.g., logic rules or ontology) that encodes spatial relationships, safety constraints, and operational policies; derive a queryable model on demand by evaluating rules against sensor observations.

DP4: Establish a real-time streaming pipeline that continuously ingests sensor data, applies feature extraction and classification, and maintains an incrementally updated spatial model; expose model state via subscription/polling interface for planning/execution.

DP5: Delegate feature capture to a hybrid approach: use pre-defined templates (e.g., site layout blueprints, CAD models) as a baseline; augment with live sensor observations to detect deviations, dynamic hazards, and unplanned features; merge into a composite model.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP5: Hybrid baseline-augmented model. Establish a composite spatial representation by anchoring to pre-defined worksite templates (blueprints, CAD, or prior maps) and augmenting with live multi-modal sensor observations to detect deviations, dynamic hazards, and unplanned features. Fuse baseline and live data into a unified, queryable geometric and semantic model. Expose model state and feature queries via a standardized interface for planning and execution consumers. Maintain model freshness and consistency through incremental updates and conflict resolution policies aligned with safety and operational constraints.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between sensor modality selection and feature extraction logic—mitigated by abstracting sensor input as a generic observation stream and keeping extraction logic modular.

Dependency on baseline template availability and accuracy—mitigated by graceful degradation: if baseline unavailable, fall back to DP1 (pure sensor fusion); if baseline inaccurate, detection of deviations triggers re-calibration.
Coupling between model update frequency and planning/execution cycle time—mitigated by decoupling via asynchronous query interface; consumers poll or subscribe at their own cadence.
Potential coupling to specific safety/operational policy encoding—mitigated by externalizing policies as queryable constraints rather than hard-coding into model structure.
coupling_check.notes: DP5 achieves loose coupling by separating concerns: baseline provision (external), live sensing (pluggable), fusion logic (centralized), and policy enforcement (declarative). The hybrid approach reduces brittleness compared to pure sensor fusion (DP1) or pure symbolic reasoning (DP3), and supports incremental model refinement without full re-acquisition.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite baseline (blueprint, CAD, or prior map) is available and reasonably accurate at mission start. RISK: If baseline is stale, missing, or significantly inaccurate, fusion may produce misleading composite model. MITIGATION: Implement baseline validation checks; flag high-confidence deviations; support manual override.

ASSUMPTION: Multi-modal sensor suite (vision, range, inertial) is available and calibrated. RISK: Sensor failures, miscalibration, or occlusions degrade feature detection. MITIGATION: Implement sensor health monitoring; use redundancy where safety-critical; graceful degradation to available modalities.

ASSUMPTION: Feature extraction and semantic labeling can be automated or semi-automated. RISK: Misclassification of hazards or features leads to unsafe planning. MITIGATION: Conservative labeling (over-report hazards); human-in-the-loop validation for high-risk features; traceability of labeling confidence.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are formalized and queryable. RISK: Policies are ambiguous, incomplete, or conflict with model representation. MITIGATION: Explicit policy schema; validation against model; escalation for unresolvable conflicts.

ASSUMPTION: Planning and execution functions can tolerate model latency and eventual consistency. RISK: Stale model leads to unsafe or inefficient decisions. MITIGATION: Define acceptable model age; implement update prioritization; alert consumers to model freshness.

ASSUMPTION: Composite model can be efficiently queried and updated in real-time. RISK: Model complexity or update frequency exceeds computational budget. MITIGATION: Hierarchical model representation; lazy evaluation; caching; spatial indexing.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP5 to explicitly separate three sub-concerns: (1) Baseline provisioning and validation (DP5a), (2) Live feature detection and anomaly flagging (DP5b), and (3) Fusion, conflict resolution, and query interface (DP5c). This decomposition clarifies ownership boundaries (IM layer) and allows independent evolution of baseline sources, sensor pipelines, and model consumers. Additionally, introduce a formal model schema (e.g., GeoJSON + semantic tags + policy constraints) to ensure interoperability and auditability. Define acceptance criteria for model freshness, feature detection recall/precision, and policy compliance.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized spatial representation engine that maintains hybrid baseline-augmented models, manages sensor fusion, handles conflict resolution, and exposes queryable geometric/semantic state to planning and execution consumers.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingests live multi-modal sensor observations (LiDAR, camera, IMU, GPS) from Vehicle and Teleoperation to augment baseline model and detect deviations/hazards.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Exposes queryable model state and feature queries (geometric and semantic) to planning and execution consumers for mission planning and real-time decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Publishes detected deviations, dynamic hazards, and unplanned features to Safety Services for constraint-aligned conflict resolution and safety enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Receives baseline templates (blueprints, CAD, prior maps) and synchronizes incremental model updates across distributed nodes to maintain freshness and consistency.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment. NGHE Solution is the natural home for spatial representation, sensor fusion, and model management. Baseline template ingestion, live sensor augmentation, conflict resolution, and queryable state exposure all align with a centralized geospatial/semantic engine. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry ingestion (Vehicle/Teleoperation) and query consumption (planning/execution) cross boundaries via standard interfaces.

Poor containment. Vehicle is a platform/agent, not a representation engine. Placing hybrid model logic here would scatter sensor fusion, baseline anchoring, and conflict resolution across vehicle instances, causing severe duplication and inconsistency. Model state would diffuse to Teleoperation, Safety Services, and planning systems.

Poor containment. Teleoperation is a control/command interface, not a persistent spatial representation layer. Embedding baseline-augmented model logic here would couple representation state to operator sessions, break model persistence, and force duplication across multiple teleoperation instances. High diffusion to Vehicle, NGHE Solution, and Safety Services.

Poor containment. Safety Services is a constraint enforcement and alerting layer, not a spatial representation engine. While Safety Services consumes model queries and enforces conflict resolution policies, it should not own the hybrid model itself. Placing representation logic here would couple safety logic to geometric/semantic fusion, violating separation of concerns. High diffusion to NGHE Solution, Vehicle, and planning systems.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the single authoritative owner of the hybrid baseline-augmented spatial model. It ingests baseline templates via Network Link, fuses live multi-modal telemetry, maintains geometric and semantic state, detects deviations and hazards, and exposes queryable model state via Tasking. Safety Services consumes model queries and enforces conflict resolution policies; Vehicle and Teleoperation provide sensor observations; planning and execution systems query the model for decision-making. This architecture centralizes representation logic, eliminates duplication, and ensures model consistency and freshness.
containment_risks: NGHE Solution may become a bottleneck if model update latency or query throughput is insufficient for real-time planning and safety-critical decision-making. Mitigation: implement incremental update batching, caching, and asynchronous query patterns.

Conflict resolution policies must be clearly defined and aligned with safety constraints. If policies are ambiguous or inconsistent, Safety Services and planning systems may diverge in their interpretation of model state. Mitigation: formalize conflict resolution rules as part of the model schema and expose them via Tasking interface.

Baseline template ingestion and versioning must be robust. If baseline updates are not properly synchronized across distributed nodes, model consistency will degrade. Mitigation: implement version control, rollback, and audit logging for baseline templates.

Multi-modal sensor fusion requires careful calibration and validation. If sensor observations are noisy, misaligned, or conflicting, augmented model state may be unreliable. Mitigation: implement sensor validation, outlier detection, and confidence scoring in the fusion pipeline.

Queryable interface must be well-specified and versioned to avoid breaking changes for planning and execution consumers. Mitigation: define a stable, extensible query schema and maintain backward compatibility.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The FR 'Capture and represent worksite spatial features in a machine-readable model' is directly verifiable against acceptance criteria: (1) worksite model/map generated and available to planning/execution functions for agreed site features, and (2) model alignment with worksite safety plan and operational policies. NGHE Solution as IM owner provides a single, observable point of verification for model generation, freshness, query availability, and safety constraint compliance.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Excellent. Allocation is unambiguous: NGHE Solution owns the hybrid baseline-augmented model end-to-end. Input flows (Telemetry, Network Link) and output flows (Tasking, SafetyAlert) are clearly defined. Ownership boundaries are crisp, with no competing claims from Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. Responsibility for sensor fusion, baseline anchoring, conflict resolution, and model state exposure is consolidated.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP5 (Hybrid baseline-augmented model) is a logically sound and complete solution to the FR. It specifies: (1) composite spatial representation anchored to pre-defined templates, (2) augmentation with live multi-modal sensor observations, (3) deviation and hazard detection, (4) unified geometric and semantic model, (5) queryable interface for consumers, and (6) freshness and consistency maintenance via incremental updates and conflict resolution. The DP is sufficiently detailed to guide design and is traceable to acceptance criteria (model availability, safety alignment). No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Sensor fusion logic (multi-modal observation integration) must remain entirely within NGHE Solution. Risk: if Vehicle or Teleoperation perform pre-processing or filtering of sensor data before ingestion, fusion state becomes distributed and inconsistent. Mitigation: define Telemetry interface as raw or minimally processed sensor streams; all fusion logic centralized in NGHE Solution.

Baseline template versioning and synchronization must be owned by NGHE Solution. Risk: if Network Link allows ad-hoc baseline updates without version control or rollback capability, model consistency degrades across distributed nodes. Mitigation: implement formal baseline schema versioning, audit logging, and atomic update semantics in NGHE Solution.

Conflict resolution policies must be formalized and owned by NGHE Solution, not delegated to Safety Services. Risk: if Safety Services interprets or applies conflict resolution rules independently, model state diverges between representation engine and constraint enforcement layer. Mitigation: expose conflict resolution rules as queryable metadata via Tasking interface; Safety Services consumes and applies rules, does not define them.

Query interface (Tasking) must be stable and versioned. Risk: if planning and execution consumers implement ad-hoc query logic or make assumptions about model state semantics, changes to NGHE Solution schema break downstream systems. Mitigation: define a formal, versioned query schema with backward compatibility guarantees.

Real-time latency and throughput constraints must be explicitly defined and validated. Risk: if model update batching or query response times exceed planning/safety decision cycles, system becomes unsafe or ineffective. Mitigation: establish SLAs for incremental update latency, query response time, and model freshness; validate against mission-critical decision timelines.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment score of 0.92 for NGHE Solution is justified. The module is the natural and only appropriate home for hybrid baseline-augmented model logic. Diffusion score of 0.08 reflects minimal boundary crossing: Telemetry ingestion (Vehicle/Teleoperation -> NGHE Solution) and Tasking queries (NGHE Solution -> planning/execution) are clean, standardized interfaces with no logic leakage. Alternative allocations (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) all exhibit severe diffusion (0.75–0.85) due to duplication, inconsistency, and violation of separation of concerns. No viable alternative to NGHE Solution ownership exists.

recommended_revisions: Formalize and document conflict resolution policies as part of the NGHE Solution model schema. Policies must be explicit, deterministic, and aligned with worksite safety plan and operational constraints. Expose policies as queryable metadata via Tasking interface so Safety Services and planning systems can validate their interpretation.

Define and enforce SLAs for model update latency, query response time, and model freshness. Establish acceptance thresholds based on mission-critical decision cycles (e.g., real-time hazard detection, planning horizon). Validate NGHE Solution architecture against these SLAs before deployment.

Specify Telemetry interface as raw or minimally processed sensor streams (LiDAR point clouds, camera frames, IMU/GPS raw data). Prohibit pre-processing or filtering by Vehicle or Teleoperation; all fusion logic must reside in NGHE Solution to ensure consistency and auditability.

Implement formal baseline template versioning, audit logging, and atomic update semantics in NGHE Solution. Define rollback and conflict resolution procedures for baseline updates received via Network Link. Ensure distributed nodes maintain synchronized baseline state.

Define a stable, versioned Tasking query schema with explicit semantics for geometric and semantic queries. Establish backward compatibility guarantees and deprecation policies to prevent breaking changes for planning and execution consumers.

Implement sensor validation, outlier detection, and confidence scoring in the fusion pipeline. Expose confidence metadata in model state queries so planning and safety systems can adjust decision thresholds based on observation reliability.

Establish clear ownership and escalation procedures for model consistency violations (e.g., conflicting baseline updates, sensor fusion failures, unresolved deviations). Define how NGHE Solution communicates model state degradation to Safety Services and planning systems.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence. The FR is well-defined, the DP is logically sound and complete, and the IM allocation to NGHE Solution is justified with minimal diffusion (0.08) and high containment (0.92). However, the recommended_revisions and diffusion_risks expose critical sub-system concerns that must be decomposed to ensure safe and reliable realization: (1) sensor fusion logic and multi-modal observation integration require detailed specification to prevent distributed state inconsistency; (2) baseline template versioning and synchronization demand formal protocols; (3) conflict resolution policies must be explicit and queryable; (4) Tasking query interface requires stable schema definition; (5) real-time latency and throughput constraints must be validated against mission-critical decision cycles. Given high safety criticality, these concerns cannot be deferred to detailed design—they must be elevated to the next decomposition level as explicit sub-FRs to ensure architectural coherence and risk mitigation before implementation. Proceeding directly to STOP would leave critical interface contracts, consistency mechanisms, and safety-critical timing constraints unspecified at the functional level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR5.1: Integrate multi-modal sensor observations (LiDAR, camera, IMU, GPS) into a unified geometric and semantic representation without loss of fidelity or introduction of distributed state inconsistency.
FR5.2: Maintain and synchronize baseline spatial templates across distributed nodes with formal versioning, audit logging, and atomic update semantics to ensure model consistency.
FR5.3: Detect and resolve conflicts between baseline templates and live sensor observations using deterministic, safety-aligned policies that are explicit, queryable, and auditable.

FR5.4: Expose spatial and semantic model state to planning and execution consumers via a stable, versioned query interface with explicit semantics, backward compatibility guarantees, and confidence metadata.

FR5.5: Validate and enforce real-time latency, throughput, and freshness constraints on model updates and queries to ensure alignment with mission-critical decision cycles (planning horizon, hazard detection response time).

FR5.6: Detect and communicate model state degradation (sensor fusion failures, baseline inconsistencies, unresolved deviations) to Safety Services and planning systems with clear ownership and escalation procedures.

revise.why: No revisions to FR, DP, or IM are required at this level. All gates pass and the allocation is sound. Revisions are captured as recommended_revisions in the gate output and will be addressed through sub-FR decomposition and detailed design.

assumptions: Telemetry interface will provide raw or minimally processed sensor streams (LiDAR point clouds, camera frames, raw IMU/GPS); Vehicle and Teleoperation will not perform pre-processing or filtering that could introduce distributed fusion state.
Network Link provides reliable, ordered delivery of baseline template updates with sufficient bandwidth and latency to support atomic synchronization across distributed nodes.
Safety Services and planning systems will consume conflict resolution policies and model state metadata via Tasking interface and will not independently interpret or apply rules.
Mission-critical decision cycles (planning horizon, hazard detection response time) are known and can be used to establish SLAs for model update latency and query response time.
Baseline spatial templates are stable and versioned; ad-hoc or uncontrolled baseline updates will not occur during mission execution.
Sensor confidence, outlier detection, and validation logic can be implemented within NGHE Solution without exceeding computational or latency budgets.
open_risks: Sensor fusion logic complexity: multi-modal observation integration (LiDAR, camera, IMU, GPS) with real-time latency constraints may exceed computational capacity or introduce undetected fusion errors. Mitigation: decompose FR5.1 to specify fusion algorithm selection, validation, and performance validation criteria.

Baseline synchronization failure: distributed baseline updates may arrive out-of-order or conflict with live sensor observations, causing model inconsistency. Mitigation: decompose FR5.2 to specify versioning protocol, conflict detection, and rollback semantics.

Conflict resolution policy ambiguity: policies may be interpreted differently by NGHE Solution and Safety Services, leading to divergent model state or safety constraint violations. Mitigation: decompose FR5.3 to formalize policies as executable rules with explicit precedence and safety alignment.

Query interface brittleness: changes to model schema or query semantics may break downstream planning and execution systems. Mitigation: decompose FR5.4 to define stable schema, versioning, and backward compatibility guarantees.

Real-time latency violation: model update batching or query response times may exceed planning/safety decision cycles, rendering system unsafe or ineffective. Mitigation: decompose FR5.5 to establish and validate SLAs against mission-critical timelines.

Model state degradation visibility: NGHE Solution may fail silently or degrade gracefully without communicating state loss to Safety Services, leading to unsafe decisions. Mitigation: decompose FR5.6 to specify degradation detection, communication, and escalation procedures.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of sensor fusion algorithm(s) and validation criteria (accuracy, latency, robustness to sensor failures).
Baseline template schema definition, versioning protocol, and synchronization semantics (atomic updates, rollback, conflict detection).
Formal specification of conflict resolution policies as deterministic rules with explicit precedence, safety alignment, and auditability.
Tasking query schema definition with explicit geometric and semantic query types, response semantics, confidence metadata, and backward compatibility guarantees.
Real-time latency and throughput SLAs derived from mission-critical decision cycles (planning horizon, hazard detection response time, safety constraint enforcement latency).
Model state degradation detection and communication protocol, including failure modes, escalation procedures, and Safety Services integration.
Computational and latency budget analysis for sensor fusion, baseline synchronization, conflict resolution, and query processing under worst-case mission scenarios.
Validation plan for NGHE Solution architecture against SLAs and safety criticality requirements before deployment.
